References
^^^^^^^^^^

1. M. Larjavaara et al. (2005). Lightning that ignited forest fires in Finland. Agricultural and Forest Meteorology. 132 (3-4) 171-180, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.agrformet.2005.07.005

2. Rorig M.L. et al. (2007). Model-Generated Predictions of Dry Thunderstorm Potential. Journal of Applied Meteorology and Climatology, 46, 605-614. https://doi.org/10.1175/JAM2482.1

3. Wickramasinghe A.M.M et al. (2024). Modeling the Probability of Dry Lightning-Induced Wildfires in Tasmania: A Machine Learning Approach, Geophysical Research Letters, 51:6, https://doi.org/10.1029/2024GL110381

4. Bae, Jinil and Wang, Simon and Yoon, Jin-Ho and Son, Rackhun, Dry CG Lightning Is Increasing in Northen Canada Because the High-Base Vertical Geometry Is Becoming More Common. Available at SSRN:  https://ssrn.com/abstract=6052007 or http://dx.doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.6052007
